attacking_style: Solo pursuit specialist on high-altitude climbs in final 5km, demonstrating elite time trial climbing capabilities under fatigue. Arrives as late-race accelerator rather than early aggressor.
team_tactics: Deployed as late-race finisher/chaser rather than primary attacker. Team shows reactive rather than proactive race reading, releasing him after gaps have formed instead of anticipating moves.
terrain_preference: High-altitude climbs requiring sustained power output; excels in solo time trial-style efforts chasing back on. Long, steady gradients where aerobic capacity dominates over explosive accelerations.
vulnerability: Team timing and positioning issues leave him chasing rather than initiating. Reactive deployment suggests struggles when races explode early or require multiple accelerations before final climb.
key_patterns: Exceptional solo chasing ability: reduced 2:20 gap to 30 seconds in 5km at altitude
Elite time trial climbing power on sustained gradients at high elevation
Late-race deployment pattern across multiple seasons, reactive not proactive positioning
Team tactical delays leave him arriving to decisive moments after gaps open
form_trajectory: unknown: 4-year gap between observations prevents trajectory assessment, though 2022 showed elite climbing capability
